news.headline: Florida's Sunshine Genetics Newborn Screening Program Launches at Tampa General Hospital With GeneDx as Sequencing Partner
news.source: Florida State University News / Inside Precision Medicine
news.summary: The nation's first state-backed genomic newborn screening program went live this summer at Tampa General Hospital, offering free voluntary whole-genome sequencing that expands screening from about 60 conditions to nearly 900, with GeneDx providing end-to-end sequencing and clinical interpretation. The program is slated to expand to hospitals in Orlando and Miami later in 2026 ahead of a statewide, five-year rollout targeting up to 100,000 newborns.

news.headline: GeneDx Downgraded by Zacks Research to "Strong Sell"
news.summary: Zacks Research cut its rating on GeneDx from Hold to Strong Sell in a note to investors, a notably more bearish call than most other analysts covering the stock. The downgrade adds to a mixed analyst picture following the company's May guidance cut.

news.headline: Guggenheim Raises GeneDx (WGS) Price Target, Maintaining a Buy Rating
news.summary: Guggenheim raised its price target on GeneDx to $74 from $70 while reiterating a Buy rating, citing the company's long-term positioning in genomic diagnostics. The move follows a string of target cuts by other analysts earlier in the quarter.

news.headline: Piper Sandler Raises GeneDx Price Target on Carelon Coverage Policy
news.summary: Piper Sandler lifted its GeneDx price target to $63 from $50, calling Carelon's newly expanded outpatient whole-genome-sequencing coverage policy a potential structural average-selling-price de-risking event, estimating it could add roughly $100 to ASP by H2 2027; shares rallied over 5% on the news.

news.headline: GeneDx Names Mark Gardner President to Lead Its Next Phase of Scale and Growth
news.summary: GeneDx appointed Mark Gardner, a former Quest Diagnostics and OmniSeq executive, as President effective June 15, 2026, to lead commercial and operations teams and strengthen execution. He reports directly to CEO Katherine Stueland.

news.headline: Hedge Funder Eli Casdin Is Loading Up on GeneDx Shares
news.summary: GeneDx board member Eli Casdin's fund Casdin Capital made further open-market purchases of GeneDx shares in early June (about $11.1 million), adding to roughly $21.5 million bought in May, after the stock fell sharply following its Q1 2026 guidance cut.

catalysts.label: Q2 2026 earnings report
catalysts.detail: GeneDx has not yet confirmed a Q2 2026 report date; based on the prior quarter's cadence, results are expected in early August. Company guidance calls for Q2 revenue of $110-112 million on roughly 30,000 tests and ~70% adjusted gross margin; investors will watch whether volume growth is translating back into margin recovery after May's guidance cut.

catalysts.label: Reimbursement/ASP recovery from Carelon coverage policy
catalysts.detail: Piper Sandler estimates Carelon's expanded outpatient whole-genome-sequencing medical-necessity criteria (effective June 14, 2026) could add roughly $100 to GeneDx's average selling price as the exome-to-genome mix shift becomes better reimbursed.

catalysts.label: ASHG 2026 Annual Meeting (Montreal)
catalysts.detail: GeneDx is expected to again showcase research drawn from its GeneDx Infinity dataset (~1 million exomes/genomes) at the American Society of Human Genetics annual meeting, as it did with 14 studies at ASHG 2025 (newborn screening/GUARDIAN, neurodevelopmental disorders, ML-driven variant classification); the 2026 abstract slate has not yet been announced.

catalysts.label: Sunshine Genetics and BEACONS newborn genomic screening expansion
catalysts.detail: Having gone live at Tampa General Hospital in July 2026, Florida's Sunshine Genetics program is slated to expand to hospitals in Orlando and Miami later in 2026 en route to a statewide five-year rollout targeting up to 100,000 newborns; GeneDx is also a sequencing partner in the separate NIH-funded BEACONS multi-state initiative, aiming to enroll up to 30,000 newborns across up to 10 states. Continued site expansion could add incremental genome-sequencing volume.

pulse: GeneDx shares remain depressed roughly two and a half months after the May 2026 Q1 report exposed a reimbursement-driven margin miss and a $31 million Fabric Genomics impairment, and the stock has since drawn a cluster of securities class-action filings (lead-plaintiff deadline August 3, 2026) that are now a background litigation overhang rather than an operational one. Against that backdrop, the tangible positive developments have been operational: the Sunshine Genetics newborn whole-genome-sequencing program went live at Tampa General Hospital in July (first real-world rollout of a state-backed genomic newborn screening program with GeneDx as sequencing partner), a new President (Mark Gardner) was brought in to sharpen commercial execution, and Carelon's expanded outpatient coverage policy has driven a string of analyst price-target increases (Piper Sandler, Guggenheim) even as others (Zacks) turned more bearish. Board member Eli Casdin has kept buying stock on the open market through June. With no Q2 2026 earnings date yet confirmed, the market's next real test of whether volume growth (guided ~30,000 tests, ~70% gross margin) is translating back into margin recovery is likely in early August.
